name: Promote The Existing 256x128 Pivot Hot-Band Kernel

summary.name: Promote The Existing 256x128 Pivot Hot-Band Kernel
summary.hypothesis: The current PTX winner surface still sits at only about 48% tensor-pipe activity, 47.8% SM throughput, and 16.6% active warps. Under the new human guidance, that means the next recommendation should not be another narrow PTX-local retime whose upside is measured in hundredths of a millisecond. The strongest aggressive live family is to promote the existing 256x128 pivot hot-band kernel and give the hot region a materially different reuse geometry. That branch is the cleanest in-tree candidate for recovering multi-millisecond headroom because it changes the hot-band shape, staging footprint, and control amortization all at once rather than chasing another tiny local ordering tweak.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Structural latency hiding and control amortization on the hot band due to undersized effective work per scheduling decision, not just one more PTX microkernel handoff seam.
summary.risk: Medium to high. This is a broad hot-band dispatch change, but that is now a feature rather than a bug because the user explicitly wants more aggressive implementations once a family is chosen.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.171520 ms restored PTX run
ranking_notes: This now ranks first because the user explicitly wants more aggressive post-selection implementations instead of another micro-retime.
The current profile still leaves enormous utilization headroom, so geometry and staging changes should outrank tiny PTX-local tweaks.
Among the live families already in-tree, this is the strongest structural branch with credible multi-millisecond upside.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the 256x128 promotion does not materially improve utilization or if it opens a broad regression without any sign of structural headroom capture.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
